| Hi Friend -
---
A reality check is in order. On the Wisconsin election, the election integrity Executive order, DOGE findings in the voter rolls, and what we have to offer the Trump Administration ... I’ll try to keep it brief.
|
---
## On Wisconsin’s election
Tuesday’s election delivered a mixed message: voters passed a constitutional amendment to codify Voter ID—while also electing a Supreme Court Justice who opposes Voter ID and other key pro-liberty principles.
Social media exploded with blame:
“Voters need to vote harder!”
“Chasers need to chase harder!”
“It must be fraud!”
But after years of work in Wisconsin—training Election Challengers, supporting Election Day operations, engaging with the WEC, and fighting for election integrity—here’s the hard truth: the biggest problem facing the pro-liberty movement is inconsistency on the ground.
You can’t just engage voters when it’s time to vote or fundraise. Real impact requires year-round connection—relationships, communication, and community. Democrats do this. Republicans don’t.
I’m not suggesting that we shouldn’t research unusual patterns in the voter rolls, nor should any grassroots group stop working in their area of focus, but ... if your local party is asleep at the wheel, tell them: lead, follow, or stop sending fundraising texts every five minutes and get out of the way. There’s too much at stake to keep pretending we’re playing the same game.
|
---
## The Awesome Executive Order
President Trump’s Executive Order _[“Preserving and Protecting the Integrity of American Elections”](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.UJcOIEsxQ3qr5lEWJDDC4n6aWBcMC8ry3MP8bGZ9oijmVt2vekhx2U6rXIw6jZI57qBqA-jFLzgM45PQlW10Vk4zZNxKkE2r0wSTkx8cOT5c6B83vqhFGjZVVWfVM3pIQYP9OKnaUWSiC5jkWdO4aMEDZ0k-Bw00e7ZOJVXxXTAscv42Mv12I1MH6dzPgBi7GdN9IZoUJtD5CjLJSG6TqFzXWFL-WGsBtKIzsJYyhMpLHBYKhm2qaQRpU8I6SMrhFWe47-PYhLgCol3QW12BYtyRNiKJSomK04BA40ujqgvkjKxH3pBv50cX9Ui7fm7UB1WBLXsHGqvMNznD89hEdhbF-ONt5S53WP1n_chvh7N9LezUJfk0hm2HIP_VcRmP6KcRE-0XMXAJJXWi7ZZ5dQ/4fg/7cLPaSCsS_GOKYxRArDt0Q/h2/h001.8fSLPGKHNdhhM7UhSAs5Q8OnRNEHcQsW-_JbPa_cUoo)_ is a thing of beauty.
It opens with: _“Under the Constitution, State governments must safeguard American elections in compliance with Federal laws that protect Americans’ voting rights and guard against dilution by illegal voting, discrimination, fraud, and other forms of malfeasance and error.”_
Yes. Exactly.
This order ends the voter registration chaos caused by Biden’s E.O. 14091, directs DHS to provide states the citizenship data needed to help clean voter rolls [(a key goal of our 611 Project—thank you to all who supported it!)](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.bYZLwP2tKNW52PF5H5Fs6XnSdcrSmzbZQfStcjy751i-Ux7pGZZnGS3_QkKO6_ELjC5yHL05Cxwb3cle8YQ6yKtVkcypgOA0jUcNT7veafcBt32r4VXADhckHug6UWEKhYIR7sg19nlnmZvzM5_hcW24iDyw2bW9XLnR_39DFb5JIPcsnyQ8nUlB7lP8tlEdpVi17R9iUw3AGqkAR-zo1GIyoQMbRdJcJlmxbpowNfm4h0M0yv8rVI2XdUooiUBdGoB1GPUddqQd_2QuvJDLBxfMT7ZiO_HZEZg3MPDFM4g/4fg/7cLPaSCsS_GOKYxRArDt0Q/h3/h001.A2UPowm4rPwFjj0TWDpjU-Oc9PjY3LGOV10JgZX1xak), and much more.
[Here’s the fact sheet on the Executive Order — well worth the read.](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.UJcOIEsxQ3qr5lEWJDDC4n6aWBcMC8ry3MP8bGZ9oiiNTT8FQ4p2iZ5HDkZF2lL3bMywwspxI0yE-6hwE4Uf_d5mn0FYy1KayiUr48xRRUWyIq3MBOVhiYC31mgNxO5TQa7YVtGelFlYm_Ca-mWZUm47nnN7O1UFgFAB1yj3DWtHPU6UaGqcnxZbLHdjGnnI3aaas6n4MDw04xXE9tyjpGau8NMbB-RQ769rUb5t6vsIaqYyYudd84-3vbpv5W4S-4oZACkg3hnqjD9VTVTUvSiN5NuH3nmylRROcLpQGl6Tb-XhxYAE4dvQHlBjki6jGjwPTEGHxcxkylCwIBxC7D9NklHfvyb3EBqwpt0AvjbwYdM4fs44idbBY_0LyjF92LoWot-GxouLLQXD7AyAaw/4fg/7cLPaSCsS_GOKYxRArDt0Q/h4/h001.AfcpsJiwiODOOsSr08QxlScNzGnjO6kUgl-WaSozasY)
This is a huge step forward. But make no mistake: [the lawsuits are already piling up.](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.UJcOIEsxQ3qr5lEWJDDC4h42CGf-_GVVWb7S3hDo4aXScAX4kE1y04U4rluZuSbqjZY9hLZRyUvOetIlsFYVrpeo6XTg9kkffNBMOT5AIVz5jiHZnWw6fV3LKFuS8HiYjfF_4KX2Yq2-Z6PFbgHtOx6QnMsjWQb_FTWW9Ps3s_DKFpGutUAPNYFRCK1bqNIn6JiTgiJ44dK2bz5xNWK26WbKl7-JPkjKcx-g5gSRutLbj53y5sxNPLAftcoUw81RkYTnPV2qvO3UgFuOM31FJtAglkyYBjlS8MetvuBzvyZ6ZSPOtoz-2s675iT8-Cqe/4fg/7cLPaSCsS_GOKYxRArDt0Q/h5/h001.spksEvjr1GKIJQtB7pHhL77Yj5meHfmgcmjT5sIbdcY) So far, 19 states and activist groups are lining up to fight it—and more will follow.
We must hold the line. Support these standards. Settle for nothing less.
True the Vote is assessing how best to engage in the legal battle ahead. This is a hill worth fighting to hold. Stay tuned.
|
---
## What DOGE is Finding ... and What Remains to be Found
As DOGE continues to report on the many, many rabbit trails that enable the exploitation of our voter rolls, it’s been interesting to watch people wake up. Here are a few clips from just one, a podcast called “All In” (which is a really good podcast).

👆End of the segment with Antonio and Chamath talking about the 2020 Election and how difficult it is to prove election fraud.
**We couldn’t agree more. To that end, we offer the following ....**
## **To the Trump Administration —**
#### We stand in full support of your efforts to improve election processes and expose election fraud. Here’s what we can provide:
* A detailed list of government databases, recommended queries, code, and supporting data to help in your efforts to identify illegal voters.
* Information on the non-citizen database first reported by Gregg Phillips—buried under whistleblower protocols that were never honored.
* Evidence provided to (and covered up by) the FBI between 2020–2022, including geospatial data showing ballot dropbox abuse.
* Details from the multi-state Konnech investigation, including the timeline leading up to my imprisonment and Gregg Phillips’— while the FBI, who knew the truth and could have helped us, instead just watched it happen.
* Copies of banking information and supporting research tied to a suspected multi-state ballot trafficking operation. We tried to bring this to the FBI, too, then we learned that, in fact, **it was us the FBI had been targeting all along.**
* Complete text and email records with FBI agents—names, contacts, everything.
* Full detail on the lawsuits against us by Elias Law and Skadden. One involves active DOJ participation. The other smears President Trump throughout.

And that’s just the beginning.
Antonio Gracias is right: he’s only seen the tip of the iceberg. We know what lies beneath.
|

clean_body: Plus ...what we saw in Wisconsin, the election integrity Executive Order, and more... | | | | April 06, 2025 | | | Hi Friend - A reality check is in order. On the Wisconsin election, the election integrity Executive order, DOGE findings in the voter rolls, and what we have to offer the Trump Administration ... I’ll try to keep it brief. | On Wisconsin’s election Tuesday’s election delivered a mixed message: voters passed a constitutional amendment to codify Voter ID—while also electing a Supreme Court Justice who opposes Voter ID and other key pro-liberty principles. Social media exploded with blame: “Voters need to vote harder!” “Chasers need to chase harder!” “It must be fraud!” But after years of work in Wisconsin—training Election Challengers, supporting Election Day operations, engaging with the WEC, and fighting for election integrity—here’s the hard truth: the biggest problem facing the pro-liberty movement is inconsistency on the ground. You can’t just engage voters when it’s time to vote or fundraise. Real impact requires year-round connection—relationships, communication, and community. Democrats do this. Republicans don’t. I’m not suggesting that we shouldn’t research unusual patterns in the voter rolls, nor should any grassroots group stop working in their area of focus, but ... if your local party is asleep at the wheel, tell them: lead, follow, or stop sending fundraising texts every five minutes and get out of the way. There’s too much at stake to keep pretending we’re playing the same game. | The Awesome Executive Order President Trump’s Executive Order is a thing of beauty. It opens with: “Under the Constitution, State governments must safeguard American elections in compliance with Federal laws that protect Americans’ voting rights and guard against dilution by illegal voting, discrimination, fraud, and other forms of malfeasance and error.” Yes. Exactly. This order ends the voter registration chaos caused by Biden’s E.O. 14091, directs DHS to provide states the citizenship data needed to help clean voter rolls , and much more. This is a huge step forward. But make no mistake: So far, 19 states and activist groups are lining up to fight it—and more will follow. We must hold the line. Support these standards. Settle for nothing less. True the Vote is assessing how best to engage in the legal battle ahead. This is a hill worth fighting to hold. Stay tuned. | What DOGE is Finding ... and What Remains to be Found As DOGE continues to report on the many, many rabbit trails that enable the exploitation of our voter rolls, it’s been interesting to watch people wake up. Here are a few clips from just one, a podcast called “All In” (which is a really good podcast). Antonio Gracias on Non Citizen Voting Pt1 ]() ☝️Antonio Gracias describing what he’s seen so far .... Results as Good as Prompts ]() 👆Host Chamath Palihapitiya explaining the importance of getting data scientists to interrogate the data. Antonio Gracias on Non Citizen Voting Pt3 ]() 👆End of the segment with Antonio and Chamath talking about the 2020 Election and how difficult it is to prove election fraud. We couldn’t agree more. To that end, we offer the following .... To the Trump Administration — We stand in full support of your efforts to improve election processes and expose election fraud. Here’s what we can provide: A detailed list of government databases, recommended queries, code, and supporting data to help in your efforts to identify illegal voters. Information on the non-citizen database first reported by Gregg Phillips—buried under whistleblower protocols that were never honored. Evidence provided to (and covered up by) the FBI between 2020–2022, including geospatial data showing ballot dropbox abuse. Details from the multi-state Konnech investigation, including the timeline leading up to my imprisonment and Gregg Phillips’— while the FBI, who knew the truth and could have helped us, instead just watched it happen. Copies of banking information and supporting research tied to a suspected multi-state ballot trafficking operation. We tried to bring this to the FBI, too, then we learned that, in fact, it was us the FBI had been targeting all along. Complete text and email records with FBI agents—names, contacts, everything. Full detail on the lawsuits against us by Elias Law and Skadden. One involves active DOJ participation. The other smears President Trump throughout. And that’s just the beginning. Antonio Gracias is right: he’s only seen the tip of the iceberg. We know what lies beneath. | Lastly, I invite you to join me on Monday night’s, where I’ll discuss all the above in much greater detail. Should be fun. 😉 | Never quit. Keep the faith. Ephesians 6 FTW! Ever onward - | P.S. We have lawsuit hearings coming up in May and June, plus we are launching in a week and finishing the build on a new app that will help track Election Integrity legislation. Any amount you choose to donate will be immediately deployed. Thank you, again and as always. | (Editor’s Note:**The email above, originally sent on April 5, mistakenly identified DOGE Volunteer and CEO of Valor Equity Partners, Antonio Gracias, as White House AI & Crypto Czar and ‘All In’ podcast host, David Sacks. The references have now been corrected.) | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- | | Update your email preferences or unsubscribe (C) 2025 True the Vote 3120 Southwest Frwy Ste 101, PMB 19128 Houston, Texas 77098-4520, United States of America

72 million Americans rely on Social Security to make ends meet.

This includes seniors, veterans and people with disabilities.

Now, unelected billionaire Elon Musk and wannabe dictator Donald Trump are gambling with their lives and attempting to gut Social Security to provide massive tax breaks to the wealthiest Americans.

Meanwhile, Democrats are fighting to expand it — by increasing annual benefits by $2,400 and ensuring it’s funded through 2096!

Please add your name before midnight tonight. | | | Right now, Elon Musk and DOGE are attempting to completely gut Social Security to line the pockets of the ultra-wealthy. That’s why Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren, Kirsten Gillibrand and top Democrats are fighting to EXPAND Social Security by $2,400 per year. PLEASE RESPOND TO OUR LIVE POLL BY 11:59 P.M.: | | | | | | | | 72 million Americans rely on Social Security to make ends meet. This includes seniors, veterans and people with disabilities. Now, unelected billionaire Elon Musk and wannabe dictator Donald Trump are gambling with their lives and attempting to gut Social Security to provide massive tax breaks to the wealthiest Americans. Meanwhile, Democrats are fighting to expand it — by increasing annual benefits by $2,400 and ensuring it’s funded through 2096! | | | | | | | | Thanks for your input, Team Gillibrand * = A note from Kirsten: Friend, I am truly honored to have won reelection, and I promise to continue the critical work toward bipartisan solutions for New Yorkers as we enter this new term. name: Sara Jacobs
subject: "As I understand it, you'll pay nothing" *Wrong*
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em]
This past week, Trump announced a sweeping set of tariffs — aka taxes — on nearly every single U.S. trading partner in a push he called “Liberation Day.”
The only thing these tariffs are liberating people from is their own hard-earned cash, since costs are about to skyrocket on basically everything we use in our daily lives.
Just a few things that will be more expensive thanks to Trump’s universal tax hikes:
* Your iPhone
* TVs
* Cars
* Clothes and shoes
* Wine, beer, and liquor
* Furniture
* Coffee
* Chocolate
And Trump either doesn’t understand this AT ALL, or is lying to the American people — or, most likely, both:
https://secure.ngpvan.com/p3FiNQpnVEOjz5INtbLOWw2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/p3FiNQpnVEOjz5INtbLOWw2]
“As I understand it, you’ll pay nothing”
I fear you don’t understand it, because that is dead wrong.
And it’s not just consumer goods that are getting pricier — housing will be hugely impacted by this move because of our reliance on imported building materials. And I know you don’t need me to tell you that we already have a housing crisis.
I’ve talked to small businesses and families in San Diego, and they all say the same thing: We can’t afford this .
The good news is that Congress could reverse this disaster. The bad news is that the GOP majority so far isn’t lifting a finger to do anything about it.
House Republicans could pass legislation to roll back the tariffs. They could hold hearings to expose the economic damage. They could push Trump to reverse course. Instead, they’re doing nothing. No pushback. No solutions. And in many cases, no town halls to answer for it.
I refuse to let GOP economic failures go unchecked. I’m fighting for trade policies that actually work — policies that lower costs, create American jobs, strengthen supply chains, bolster American manufacturing, and keep our international alliances intact. But with Democrats in the minority, we need to do more than just fight in Congress.
We need public pressure.
Will you help? When enough of us raise our voices, we make it clear that people are watching — and that we won’t accept bad policies without a fight.
📢 Sign my petition now to say NO to Trump’s reckless tariffs. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/p3FiNQpnVEOjz5INtbLOWw2]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://secure.ngpvan.com/p3FiNQpnVEOjz5INtbLOWw2]
Thanks for speaking out,
Sara
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em]
Congresswoman Sara Jacobs
California’s 51st District

body:
Yes - the rumors are true. I've got some major news for you!
Yes - the rumors are true. I've got some major news for you!
| |
---
| | | | [](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/FepLcUu13tcgidjeRMQWa5s3eaTTNIW9r9i8qPWeONuE93RNt9Cjtm913FayjprfV0KWlh6b-zEBxeIubCvTOjhlx9Hmg3972XMFfJ-AT4_zUppQmoEB2m-7lrlrlwdtfw090xCAvyMfpeP9JfVp5EaIw3lbTd4DzXUd022ivFL5BmIIYs6Anq0RiMys3cpOZAjZWHD_qjjtCy8vBm709upt0_arIPLj048zwKw1z8ArihLFI3uvKZTbtyA9ql8KhQKck-KRLsOvEsqj41W8EKjtYDLfaQLFQZxuUPxG-UBISHXPbY2iMfLq7swbH_zhHnwo3ADpQ1kjX6BND2FWDObv9NM9Dkdk8iHjxSpQ1Vc5sUb_J1tKgDHvUABcqGQDvgqTyEU8xtByHD5zOdRKgchppfjZn_vUfpU7Wp_LYl0f0__EzR19ip5T2j3UPsNral2_g62_zQ7c1Liwmf2Hp8S1deS7uNqtDn7LgyDvk4PjXY6kllZpeNBzaRtwPshjQHs/p9JXZq5qN8fd24_w70dVBdyL2hCMwkGK/7)
---
Hi, it's Vivek Ramaswamy.
---
**[In a moment, I'm going to ask you to become a Founding Donor to this historic campaign - but first, I'd like to explain why if you'll indulge me for a moment.](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/jagVsXDsQMdGqVLLUhGoGcszPOg_H34pwONkiymrtDexJ6ZSMH21aKAfzfJCcL4AwI9uJgdQIH8nlGNAgI4U3Z0GSXPE8RYHeeB_i-UzyCMG7YuANOdc5YQPfueOh6OsVjr1R9kQ9q39p1jdRvw-GmABF-5WYDWPLpYXxYwAaCZecEkwt9iXzM9dQSU9z9rtkzfoMsf4WNEVjPyJT58V5B6MV2-5CHYgehINb17TKBrteh8aPBM4maHI4y8u5AFi3uqxidyPBChHzrDCv_0jEErTK_qDNdgrKuiI1te24WldBZ7ovsjPnvhF6UF2_FtqnARGR7X8YeMDZCBiT4n1WeaApN9_OOVNhvSR9YlO8TtVTDa5Zl-D4LAxdtLufIyil5YcgILHcvS_wbeh5O-dtdBL0xbZWY2LNbzck7oe3knJteIe_56g2XyE8XEk8-4pOJmwp-UE_ymDDTmlpnsjHS2ubuxMrQagbB85TRrgf9Hb14qOnuoeYKxItJeyVmsWMac/N9G9_zuECj6D6kwO2o7fj5I1xVVTe36i/7 "In a moment, I’m going to ask you to become a Founding Donor to this historic campaign – but first, I’d like to explain why if you’ll indulge me for a moment.")**

---
** WHY RUN NOW? **
---
President Trump winning re-election in November was not just another political victory - it was a seismic realignment for our country. He won every swing state. He improved his vote share of minorities and young voters. We won the House AND Senate. And heck, he even won the popular vote.

---
| [](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/cdiQ_94090jnaMDokB7pBWMlQg51247MNlYLq5W3qaWP-o97DHR66Y61CnpfNlYkl8eDjYpziC_bJcjmd6r-qEUJaNm77HN5G9EDy2U4I-tuYSIbGLWx4roLki4MId_DaMSmjG5nZTtvnn61xhSFcNPfBDtodKV8d8y38Pb4BNmWtnrRz8gY-fTnRzYZ5GupIFVSMjl9W1D_pEh7rYUAI5arePwdN2IwB_E0vEVO0RUTjqsIIbssiX_R6h3IyDpl3qxvmiIwaviPI2y8hriiQEL6LlaDOGs4xaKUjX-k8zhpVdkspmKtuQcVSOOKc8nAnozi3ZVFACyVo22SyLFYCdLi60lGPeMv5Vt8ePyTAnZcD7cCBF6bXjky59mLRLc8gEoQZk5kEhbGVbe6Y1KttyPHe6h9-8w33DAtCHxScZl4TwKj9iKumm3ZA1iI5F_Se2OIvs138IGtM84K5ss6BBIK9XuYDrqqErqQDU0uB9mTckHtARDSkWuB3svy1xQxg6s/kzHqnDiA6tjtbVgvgl79AWb9_cWeGy-g/7)
---
It was the honor of a lifetime to rally voters in battleground states, campaign on college campuses to turn out Gen-Z for Trump, and promote his America First vision at rally after rally.

---
| [](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/aV-FuzyBDaHQCoVrumnIv3hBgmVWJUQMfnJ9io-vgcVRJVsesULwbODASWueHQmAvQzENfn5CFckIk_hx-LTI_CopW1owtX-1AN3hLhn6goCR_Ylr1X2_PgAEeTlalPV2_5gUiYxfcsDU8MQzivOWEhJGKsUPuPFAVXasmauXgkzTNevky3kMcdh8rlSU9SHfA81BR8rIdKxwthNUlNznXEv3RPLX5YPV3tgHepRh9K_mEMzuYypaqRUYOtQTLIhMdaM4Vb4-MiMSk11YTGmIbayiRhVmJqVSTTV9l7Eu2oyTV-E7-zoLQ3HR3zqX46y45DCKZ_fDwmgDmCeeCfc4oiK8Rj2fRTowwl-yvHwy2ueKJUVOnhSUjq6g8rnlloSJFqQqTSN3inS1x_D0iWWfHqUEyTCv3ofo19Z-hx1HNd5pmq2nNPkpKqbJDn0JJT1MfCpd9gdp3I2JUsqVMg2tKXKJjfbKfGjIp1M7AlCuZPQBPvWPvDx2rx-65vJi23Hpwk/tGXZ8wYQ1LIj-9B37U0Y5gWMclqMVjb3/7)
---
Right now, we have a once-in-a-generation chance to make real, lasting change to the country we know and love. It's an opportunity to not just ticker around the edges of the Federal Government, but to upend it completely.

This is our moment to GUT the managerial bureaucracy, FIRE anyone connected to the weaponization of our own government against us, SLASH the size and scope of the administrative state, and usher in the GOLDEN AGE for America!

But the Deep State is already putting up the fight of its life. They're resisting change at every turn. Make no mistake - we can't afford to squander this historic opportunity. That's why President Trump needs trusted partnerships at the state level RIGHT NOW!

---
** WHY OHIO? **
---
This one's easy. I was born and raised here in the Buckeye State and graduated from St. Xavier - a Catholic high school in Cincinnati. My wife, Apporva, and I live outside Columbus, raising our two boys here. I've started multiple businesses in Ohio. I've also known JD Vance since college and campaigned all over the state for our excellent new Senator, Bernie Moreno.
---
| [](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/lDIWgB4i_4NzqpNxycw1dhM0s7JB2Nm7yrxgGLJpklizCJki9tlapqzazpwWNnzsj6S8kWk-yqKQmd23rcsioX2okPHw3QYrPoKJU4acCqKZ0eqTbl-L9FTtavuvd8mmXhosTQgQzKrD1A-jLd5AsjhXyfGDbL8_ClEUN313FkiLxmn9jyP97zAA8Thrn-yYSs_0EuOmYpYGeWQAi6pmQU2eUcnRIiSUu3uoKUpm6oqodfMiYDUTjjyNbcnlodEf1eOxhIj9CBQmVYCX-yJ45weXzoyh-Khn8L2dmP55WVLRz9C9NSaHvgcvqlnU-h2vxQyWsZ4pqCijBjakb3hxSR-wYXwrop5k5nhBhCOkWregGgwXVOLIryZ-alH_rnLNmbLBN1yO4eUCM9vAH3W2ebgR6dFQTQzsc3zAKb0Soha_tJUrnjONwtXuKAfP9rl3z4pBBY50DMmsqI-4wUfNiSQIC8FNhjq0_gAGhBr_MXNV9D4MsCQqa53Q2zzD6Id1bVU/zhLxnkeWfkxWCtQO2zoIU3C2_0F3kgSu/7)
---
The people here are honest, have steel in their spines, and embody the City on a Hill spirit that our country was founded upon. [As Governor, I'd be honored to represent them and the values they stand for.](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/fEhZmpGs9jum3O_fjIftUh40Gptpf0z_kOc6-DIpcFgUPuPhNx90chognhz3OMO2zJ2NwPrvxTOojyctwryZoFonrRqIB0MMsXHAxYNFQfHlCfbHIFxHaR5M5UnCKNSy8xZpjeY_u16aRAbaSrYS9WHn1iAnd5FaoJVyW-DSX085GzgtPcv1HLPPqmYxHkHv72rGlJbYl1qEffzTeqSa999Tlsa6MJowS3MzCK8O9utTGW1VsnRh5Xk7GX5t4RDRTy6soN1ANBqkEn3LVr_r8WYJyP0inFlqZIqKnENx5WhCGlg2TqlTUHcnzWSt1LJRHVk9wBTnhWhGpugJ278REYQOetBKmGysMkRixDfWkusF_1mfXq6G2lrQfvy5rW858XiVc7dh8FYXI-5fa7dSl9ew2FdEdhg45iW9EJreFUiwmGFKXZkXQMwnHmELwVVVzI-iCjoto4UJVOz0I0_F-l7RzvGuYPt17skFyQeOkMqKPC6lJ-uE3ktRuF1CDjSqs9ZjlifoK-cH57IrHDjdhTjKTUD3w3Y/G_PUqMz3UjZVNYV0EMbaxv96DsOORTZd/7 "As Governor, I’d be honored to represent them and the values they stand for.")
---
| [](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/HZClPAgC-56yJHs4rC0P1hdKO1jUJl4d-SErWj2hdorCvLL4dP4Xh9fG8bcLruzRmHJT_R4QaiZG4AVkUKuXeFVVHmvuINrOFdJAy6p2RXY2njh0kWlcpBAIzxLxuKH6E9QoLs0kg2WogIdmsGmj7OfUU2XuGn6Q0U4Ou6Zlh3byTEI86rQzs58hcsGjeveG9961_XLJ7Zx34_-TQHTSTj3sFF8y6y96LlvfveK2ouI9MYqdkGqC5xxWs3jF3PqxorvgBhag3Fe6blHYgSso__hEnqLk3GRyuGN8B_sceHipRsiNfSbRF-XRggs2Hbi2SiwZHViWlliUpqh9vdW9_WYkydBvXxmaK5Sj1WQyku8O-ce4gX1LDhYk6hjrlx5xou6bVjLGdUivjShBZAEsXGQQmP8an1rplckUktYhbRCDmg3Zj4OX4cgfAj7ixJsLIZXcUB5qDk5GgfGJJ8u5tYVIcm9uMxTblNw05pccHwYc-cyMsuF8dSUXB0BDjDh6C3s/EHcysQXnDs6mDPU47hzIvZc_oblhGYVm/7)
---
** WHY GOVERNOR? **
---
Having traveled across the country, met with hundreds of politicians, ran multiple businesses, and seen how deals really get cut in The Swamp, I have never been more certain that REAL change only gets done with buy-in at the executive level.

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 4/6/2025
body:
Radical bills, Capitol protests & a new direction for the CO GOP
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s6m16ac6bbf)
---
**April 6, 2025** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST** **By Heidi Ganahl**
| | Dear Readers,
We’re starting this week with a bit of good news: Heidi’s surgery was successful, and she is home and recovering well! She’s incredibly grateful for your prayers, support, and kind messages during this time. She’ll be back soon—stronger than ever. 💪
Now, on to this week’s top stories. _And what a week it’s been._
The Colorado GOP’s newly elected leadership team—Chair Brita Horn, Vice Chair D. Lee Phelan Sr., and Secretary Russ Andrews—has hit the ground running with a message of unity, strategy, and renewed focus on winning elections.
But as often happens with big transitions, [some challenges remain](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sak14bde5c6). A recently filed motion from a subcommittee formed under the previous administration—the CRSPCIC—is attempting to continue litigation that the current State Central Committee (SCC) has moved to dismiss. The new leadership views this as a distraction from the mission of rebuilding and refocusing the party, while others believe it represents unresolved issues that still need to be addressed.
Secretary Russ Andrews struck a [unifying tone](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s7lf4a98ca1), reminding everyone that “Dave Williams and Anna and Hope, they’re not our enemies,” and urged members to remember who the real opponents are: the progressive establishment controlling our state. His message echoed the central theme of the reorganization meeting: if we want to win, we must stop fighting each other and start fighting for Colorado.
While Republicans work to unify internally, the battle outside the Capitol is intensifying.
This weekend, [thousands of protesters](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/saibf566230) gathered at the State Capitol and across Colorado as part of the national “Hands Off!” demonstrations, opposing the Trump administration’s efforts to cut federal agencies, scale back government programs, and implement major policy reforms.
Inside the Capitol, things weren’t much better. A slew of [radical, far-left bills](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sch78df0812) continue to move at breakneck speed through the legislature—targeting [parental rights](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s7t44988f7c), the [Second Amendment](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sbj4106d2af), and [TABOR](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s8hff6972e9). The political class in Denver is moving fast, and they are not playing fair.
Chair Horn summed it up best: “If we’re all standing around blaming each other while the house is burning down, we’re not putting the fire out.”
There’s a clear sense that the grassroots are ready for the fire to be put out—for good.
We’ll continue to report fairly and thoroughly on developments inside the party as it charts its new course. For now, the focus is on healing, rebuilding, and doing the hard work it takes to make Colorado great again.
In liberty and truth, The RMV Team
---
|
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[95,000 petitions delivered to the Capitol, urging Polis to veto SB25-003](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sdkec4aa46b)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/seo0be3b81a) Between Rocky Mountain Gun Owners (RMGO) and the Colorado State Shooting Association, nearly 95,000 petitions were delivered on Thursday calling on Governor Jared Polis to veto SB25-003, the controversial “safety training” bill that critics say imposes unconstitutional restrictions on the Second Amendment.
**[Rewriting the rules: Wolves, federal reform and a lawsuit from rural Colorado](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s1oe50da473)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s3o00838168)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sarb1aff13b) Late last year, five wolves were airlifted from Oregon to Colorado under a plan voters narrowly approved—but few knew one of them came from a pack with a history of livestock attacks. Fewer still knew the move may have violated federal law.

**[Cole: Colorado’s gender bills sideline parents and rush kids into harm](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/seu841eb032)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s2h059c6aa6) On Friday, March 28, Colorado legislators introduced House Bill 25-1309 and House Bill 25-1312, proposals crafted to safeguard transgender youth. Their aims are well-meaning: one mandates insurance coverage for gender-affirming treatments such as hormone therapy and surgeries, while the other anchors gender identity within custody law to protect vulnerable children.
|
---
**LEGISLATIVE SESSION**
**[Davis: Democrat lawmakers target faith as discriminatory under HB25-1312](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s1sddf5544a)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s4gf5233f78)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s1v3fd3110b) Colorado is rapidly becoming one of the nation’s most “progressive” states. And by “progressive,” I mean “radically opposed to God, family, life, marriage, and basic human biology and aggressively hostile to anyone who holds historic conservative and Christian beliefs on these issues.”
**[AGNC: Democrats’ TABOR lawsuit a “legislative betrayal” against Colorado taxpayers](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sftb3e7fce9)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s0jc1f3fe1e)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s3l9a444c14) Democrats in the Colorado legislature are pushing forward with a plan that would fundamentally alter the state’s tax system—starting with a lawsuit aimed at dismantling the Taxpayer’s Bill of Rights (TABOR).
**[Hancock: SB25-086 tramples the Constitution in the name of control](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s7q2a8a1bfb)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s8u0162a286)****[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s7q052d4ed8)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s8q6688251c) In our political age where virtue signaling often masquerades as policymaking, Colorado Senate Bill 25-086, charmingly titled Protections for Users of Social Media, stands as a masterclass in misdirection.
**[Polis greenlights nuclear power as Colorado rejects “Green New Deal”](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sbi37b64beb)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s3mce25eecd) After years of progressive opposition to nuclear energy, Colorado Governor Jared Polis has signed House Bill 25-1040 into law, officially recognizing nuclear power as part of the state’s clean energy portfolio.
**[Second scandal, same party: staff mistreatment allegations mount for Colorado Democrats](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s6se5323122)** [COLORADO PEAK POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s2pa0a47f1b) It’s only been a few weeks since Sonya Jaquez Lewis resigned from the Colorado Senate amidst accusations that she mistreated her young employees.
**[READ MORE LEGISLATIVE SESSION UPDATES](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sck103e6817)**
|
---
**STATE**
**[Griswold sues against Trump’s executive order on elections but is a case study for it](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sam068286cf)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/satb8766e3d)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sdlb06082c1) When Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold announced that she was joining a multi-state lawsuit to block President Trump’s Executive Order (EO) on election integrity, she declared the action a defense of democracy itself.
**[Phil Weiser puts politics over Constitution in legal war against President Trump](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sahb4a1c61b)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sfn1094ff84)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sema7a99d26) Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser is once again putting his progressive ideology ahead of the Constitution, launching a string of lawsuits aimed at blocking President Donald Trump’s executive orders—orders designed to restore law and order, secure the border, and protect minors from irreversible medical harm.

**[A shepherd in the trenches: Rep. Scott Bottoms answers the call to fight for Colorado](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s0naa35137a)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sfvec76a64d)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s5p0359a825) El Paso County Republican Rep. Scott Bottoms walked the quiet State Capitol halls with his wife, prayers filling the empty space. They spent hours there—no fanfare, just a pastor seeking God’s will on a new path after years of preaching in Colorado Springs.

_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s5ha4db5972)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[Education Department cracks down on schools that keep secrets from parents](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s8s33102936)**
**[](The%20left-wing%20Brennan%20Center%20for%20Justice%20claimed%20that%20more%20than%2020%20million%20Americans%20%E2%80%9Clack%20ready%20access%E2%80%9D%20to%20proof%20of%20citizenship%20documents.%20What%E2%80%99s%20more,%20the%20group%20asserted%20that%20survey%20data%20shows%20nearly%204%20million%20Americans%20%E2%80%9Cdon%E2%80%99t%20have%20these%20documents%20at%20all.%E2%80%9D)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/scle02f6be8) The U.S. Department of Education under Secretary Linda McMahon has announced a sweeping effort to enforce existing federal laws that protect parental rights in public schools. The March 28 directive reaffirms parents’ authority over their children’s education and mental health records—specifically targeting public school policies that have kept parents in the dark.
**[Republicans win special house elections, expanding slim majority](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s7hd514290c)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sfh750d3469)**[THE FEDERALIST](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sbi92e3a330) Republican candidates won both special House elections in Florida on Tuesday night, expanding the party’s slim majority in the lower chamber.

**[Liberation day and the end of the world’s trade war against America](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s4l4b9c8808)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s6v50cf6645)**[BREITBART](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s9m08976505) Trump has dubbed it Liberation Day—a piece of branding with more substance than cynicism. For weeks, he’s signaled that a sweeping new tariff regime is coming. Twenty percent across the board? A reciprocal system that mirrors foreign rates? Something hybrid and more complex? The ambiguity is not a bug. It’s leverage.
**_[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s4rcc388ca0)_**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Rushed or responsive? Home rule proposal catches Douglas County off guard](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s7r316ae42b)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s1t9ed331fc)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s6s0def1b65) On March 25, the Douglas County Board of Commissioners voted unanimously to initiate a nine-step process to become a home rule county. If approved, Douglas would join Weld and Pitkin as the only counties in Colorado with a home rule charter.

**[‘I can’t do business in Denver now’: Developers flee as climate mandates bite](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s2lc529eaf8)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s4qc25dbf8f)**[DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sbo6c04a125) While Colorado is earning praise from climate advocates for its new mandates to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, developers and their economists are giving D and F grades to the state and its capital city, blaming the regulations for a noticeable decline in some projects.

**[Pueblo sees uptick in fentanyl use](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sbl7d863990)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s0r698bbfc5)**[KKTV](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/seud1487e1b) The Pueblo Department of Public Health and Environment (PDPHE) monitors the use of drugs, like fentanyl, by testing wastewater. According to their wastewater dashboard, last month fentanyl use in Pueblo increased for the first time this year.
**[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sehc698b4e9)**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[Devotional: Perseverance – Keep Climbing! It’s Worth It!](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sdhee7138bb)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s1s329f5ebb)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s8r3cb737f1) _Ever found yourself halfway up a mountain thinking, Why did I do this? Your legs burn, your lungs gasp, and your motivation runs dry. The summit seems distant, and the parking lot below is tempting._
**[Proctor: Colorado’s conservative comeback starts at the ballot box](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sduad474d1d)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s9l5468f791)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/scm6dc4b1a3) _One of the greatest challenges facing conservatives in Colorado is voter turnout. We have seen this time and time again. While progressive groups have successfully mobilized their base, many conservatives fail to vote regularly, leaving elections decided by a mobilized opposition._
__
**[Gaines: Colorado Democrats blew the budget and now they want your TABOR refunds](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sfr6696a332)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s5hccfff693)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/saj65ffd989) _Colorado Democrats made a hash of the state’s finances, but they want YOU to pay to fix it. In this post I want to flesh out some of the many assaults on TABOR and fiscal responsibility the Democrats are cooking up for this legislative session._
__
**[Rahn: Trump’s election order restores faith in voting](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s9i76066fe2)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/s2t56dce606)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/seldb11225f) _As a Frederick Douglass Republican and former Vice Chair of the Colorado GOP, I’ve always believed that a fair and transparent electoral system is the foundation of our nation. President Trump’s recent executive order, “Preserving and Protecting the Integrity of American Elections,” addresses critical vulnerabilities in our election system—vulnerabilities that have eroded trust and threatened the integrity of our democracy._
**[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._Mq9w/sfu87c31f9f)**
|
---
**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming a RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [tori@rockymountainvoice.com)](mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\)) or send us a message on social media to chat!
|
---
**🌋 DID YOU KNOW?** While Mount Everest holds the title for the tallest mountain above sea level at 29,029 feet, Hawaii’s Mauna Kea is **actually taller** when measured from base to peak. Rising about 33,500 feet from the ocean floor—though only 4,205 feet appear above sea level—Mauna Kea is the true giant when you count its full height beneath the waves.
|

**QUOTE OF THE DAY** “Let others lead small lives, but not you. Let others argue over small things, but not you. Let others cry over small hurts, but not you. Let others leave their future in someone else’s hands, but not you.” –Jim Rohn

After years of progressive opposition to nuclear energy, Colorado Governor Jared Polis has signed House Bill 25-1040 into law, officially recognizing nuclear power as part of the state’s clean energy portfolio. It’s only been a few weeks since Sonya Jaquez Lewis resigned from the Colorado Senate amidst accusations that she mistreated her young employees. | STATE When Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold announced that she was joining a multi-state lawsuit to block President Trump’s Executive Order (EO) on election integrity, she declared the action a defense of democracy itself. Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser is once again putting his progressive ideology ahead of the Constitution, launching a string of lawsuits aimed at blocking President Donald Trump’s executive orders—orders designed to restore law and order, secure the border, and protect minors from irreversible medical harm. El Paso County Republican Rep. Scott Bottoms walked the quiet State Capitol halls with his wife, prayers filling the empty space. They spent hours there—no fanfare, just a pastor seeking God’s will on a new path after years of preaching in Colorado Springs. | NATIONAL The%20left-wing%20Brennan%20Center%20for%20Justice%20claimed%20that%20more%20than%2020%20million%20Americans%20%E2%80%9Clack%20ready%20access%E2%80%9D%20to%20proof%20of%20citizenship%20documents.%20What%E2%80%99s%20more,%20the%20group%20asserted%20that%20survey%20data%20shows%20nearly%204%20million%20Americans%20%E2%80%9Cdon%E2%80%99t%20have%20these%20documents%20at%20all.%E2%80%9D The U.S. Department of Education under Secretary Linda McMahon has announced a sweeping effort to enforce existing federal laws that protect parental rights in public schools. The March 28 directive reaffirms parents’ authority over their children’s education and mental health records—specifically targeting public school policies that have kept parents in the dark. Republican candidates won both special House elections in Florida on Tuesday night, expanding the party’s slim majority in the lower chamber. Trump has dubbed it Liberation Day—a piece of branding with more substance than cynicism. For weeks, he’s signaled that a sweeping new tariff regime is coming. Twenty percent across the board? A reciprocal system that mirrors foreign rates? Something hybrid and more complex? The ambiguity is not a bug. It’s leverage. | LOCAL On March 25, the Douglas County Board of Commissioners voted unanimously to initiate a nine-step process to become a home rule county. If approved, Douglas would join Weld and Pitkin as the only counties in Colorado with a home rule charter. While Colorado is earning praise from climate advocates for its new mandates to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, developers and their economists are giving D and F grades to the state and its capital city, blaming the regulations for a noticeable decline in some projects. The Pueblo Department of Public Health and Environment (PDPHE) monitors the use of drugs, like fentanyl, by testing wastewater. According to their wastewater dashboard, last month fentanyl use in Pueblo increased for the first time this year. | COMMENTARY Ever found yourself halfway up a mountain thinking, Why did I do this? Your legs burn, your lungs gasp, and your motivation runs dry. The summit seems distant, and the parking lot below is tempting. One of the greatest challenges facing conservatives in Colorado is voter turnout. We have seen this time and time again. While progressive groups have successfully mobilized their base, many conservatives fail to vote regularly, leaving elections decided by a mobilized opposition. Colorado Democrats made a hash of the state’s finances, but they want YOU to pay to fix it. In this post I want to flesh out some of the many assaults on TABOR and fiscal responsibility the Democrats are cooking up for this legislative session. As a Frederick Douglass Republican and former Vice Chair of the Colorado GOP, I’ve always believed that a fair and transparent electoral system is the foundation of our nation. President Trump’s recent executive order, “Preserving and Protecting the Integrity of American Elections,” addresses critical vulnerabilities in our election system—vulnerabilities that have eroded trust and threatened the integrity of our democracy. | Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? We'd love to talk with you about becoming a RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\) or send us a message on social media to chat! | 🌋 DID YOU KNOW? While Mount Everest holds the title for the tallest mountain above sea level at 29,029 feet, Hawaii’s Mauna Kea is actually taller when measured from base to peak. Rising about 33,500 feet from the ocean floor—though only 4,205 feet appear above sea level—Mauna Kea is the true giant when you count its full height beneath the waves. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | QUOTE OF THE DAY “Let others lead small lives, but not you. Let others argue over small things, but not you. Let others cry over small hurts, but not you. Let others leave their future in someone else’s hands, but not you.” –Jim Rohn | | | | ---|---|--- Did a friend forward you this email? | A product of Rocky Mountain Voice, Inc. 8200 S Quebec St, STE A3601 Centennial, CO 80112 This message was originally sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com Be sure to put “RockyMountainVoice.com” on your safe-senders whitelist. Print more money... borrow more... kick the can down the road.

name: Ernest Lineberger
subject: Hands Off was great! Here’s another chance this Sunday, April 13th!
body:
*The Democrats in Sanger (North Denton County on I-35) are hosting another
rally / protest in Sanger Square this Sunday, April 13th. This one’s in
the afternoon with much better weather. Starts at 1:30 PM.*

Hi! I’m Ernest Lineberger, your Democratic candidate running to flip our
seat in the US House currently occupied by Republican Trump supporter
Brandon Gill. I helped lead the very successful one-hour Hands Off protest
rally in Flower Mound’s Parker Square two days ago. The turnout, the
attitude, the comradery was awesome. This was only one of thousands
nationwide giving a strong message to the administration that we are
watching and not happy with what they are doing.

*This Sunday we have another chance to show that we are still here.*

This is a more rural area of our district. Let’s join in and share our
message with our more rural neighbors. We need to welcome every voter to
our cause, regardless of who they voted for last time. We need to help
them see how badly this is going and we need to vote the Dem’s in and let
them lead.

[image: image.png]

Here is the link for the event details:

https://fb.me/e/5tAOFScaR

Same rules apply as with the Hands Off.

We will be safe.

Our signs and language will be clean and family friendly with no calls for
bad activity.

We will join together. We will be seen and heard. We will be 100%
peaceful and respectful.

We will leave the area better than when we arrived.

We will not give the news anything to show negatively about us.

Our message will be the news.

Here are a couple pics from the Flower Mound Hands Off Rally.

[image: image.png]

Putting People’s Needs Before Wealthy Greed and Party Politics!

Ernest Lineberger

Candidate for US House of Representatives for Texas CD26

LinebergerforUScongress.com

name: American Briefing
subject: Elon Warns of $36T Crisis - Trump Scrambling For A Fix
body:
Blackrock warns "Prepare for a recession unlike any other." Central banks igniting a global recession with their fight to stop inflation.
Now, he’s sounding the alarm about America’s $36 trillion debt time bomb that could destroy the fabric of our society.

| |
---
---
# Elon Musk Warns of America’s $36 Trillion Dollar Debt Bomb
# The system is crumbling, protect your wealth or suffer the fallout.
---
Elon Musk has avoided two major financial crises before. He pulled Tesla and SpaceX back from the brink of collapse and built two of the most valuable companies in history.

Now, he’s sounding the alarm about America’s $36 trillion debt time bomb that could destroy the fabric of our society.

As head of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) under President Trump, Musk is exposing just how bad things are:

✅ Runaway government spending has pushed national debt to unsustainable levels
✅ The Federal Reserve’s rate hikes are squeezing the economy, making inflation irreversible
✅ The stock market is on shaky ground, putting traditional 401(k)s, IRAs, and TSPs at risk

With Trump back in charge, major spending cuts are coming. While necessary, these cuts may send shockwaves through Wall Street, creating unpredictable market turbulence.

That’s why financial elites aren’t waiting to react, they're moving their wealth now.

For the everyday American who’s worked hard to build their nest egg, Trump preserved a IRS loophole that allows you to protect your retirement savings before billions in American wealth are lost.

# Monday, April 7, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1969, the Defense Department's Advanced Research Projects Agency published the first digital document on a shared computer network, which is often regarded as the symbolic [birth date of the Internet](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z7FugzAQANCvsbeg8_kMZPBQqfLUdO2IDBzhWoMjsGj5-6rd8wNPeh-37hYlyXp_jwv7MuUOAd0F6AJNN0ZJZ0d69BMzstXsTUMECGhqPft6sBE4RtOysX3fooVrc2VjmMj05LT4Pw0IGgMAhJVxHMG21LNzA9dOEfAoJW_VZ5a1zDylMx-8VUNedPJzKY9d2ReFQWGYZS95O_c1Dl97JVlh4IPXojC8viG4IfwoDHrz4-NbUpK9bhTBfYmS_r3y7Hd4_A0AAP__5zpQfA). Today, we're spotlighting 94-year-old Judith Viorst, who takes us through [decades of reinvention](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z7FuwyAQgOGngQ3rfBwmHhi6eEvXjhY1R0xKQoRJLL99le6df-mT_q_zfPYpp_vl09_YtVhmBDQKSIGdg0_5mEkGF5mRtWTXWyJAwH6Qq1t8MMTMeoQYmWIc0Z70AsMIw8mSlsm9NSCwPQAQdr1hD_pE32zMwoMRBBxSK7W7lnRvK8d8lBfXbik3md3a2mMT-kPgJHDa973zOT8rv7PAaWulHgKn6zOktqpXKnVrKpfysynf1EiyuvDYU85pG6wguNx8yn92--_15fA3AAD__385VkU) in a charming essay that proves personal style only gets better with age. Today's sponsor is the [inner-city ministry of Cru®](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kU3LnDAUhX_NdafEmw9fFy7KiKtO6aJQugoxuTopiRk0WubfFwe7KbzLPJw8HO75edd344Nf5m8mUpenpJGhLJkoWaOd8eGlReG6iQiJF9TVjRAMGdaqeHScW2pNO06jNKolhaMlIWs5MiXxg0Thu9PGBGtqxpjAqpZkGP8QI0lpSUkQjJzPaa1-J7_kB03hlQ5aK5tiEbpHzs8N-BfAAXCY_UGVXfcqrTPg8FbzksyWaR3NvFWPHAPwISZnAvD-zJeznzKgcsB7xAZb1QAqe77-_w6obiY-jZ-XW3IEvP8x8OHrL0C156i3tK_2pMZuF4rk_B6B91frC9vLArz_R9KSacln0j51rV2sGWualpfH2Y2CP2h9nRMA74fbd13r_n5FirVzzz8-BL-pBgSbo_HhfZ_82V5Hh38DAAD__31JktA). This Easter, they're bringing joy to children in underserved communities--and you can be part of it. Join their mission today.

## Trade Negotiations in Full Swing over Tariffs
At least [50 countries](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2O2zAQhJ-G6tZY_kiOCxa-BOouzRUpDZpcWYwlrkBSEvT2gdJfNcAM8AHz_fl8fLo4xfT67WaydeCHQtUCGsDrI7g4HQ_TBDsQKdINWXk1BhUq2TWjJSMHdfMumGcXpOyeN6OfErWWTqvbDZtoTxoavEpENOoiW3Kof5gnta2nrhUGKcTK-fKXY6ojDdPBG-WL57mZ7FjrUoS-C9UL1e_7fplLOjehekqwFqH6mRMdZ7r8pno2Na_zUqDyAuQ58Rw9uLDFQhmKOwq0CJ7XVHOkAqPbCDI5P1IAXitUhkQvrtFVgupyHIaT6jLc7_Inv7--hO7ZxyD0r4_THO3lI_NeqMk2LHucpli6qzD4ml2c_l-p36ndrPoXAAD__8vDfWM) have approached the Trump administration to negotiate new trade deals as a wave of international tariffs begins to take effect. Vietnam, which sells far more to the U.S. than it buys in return--making it one of the largest contributors to America's trade gap--said Sunday that it is "ready" to [remove all tariffs](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kT2PnDAQQH-N6WY1_sBwBcVeIrpLm3I14OHWiY2R7QXx7yO2T_vm6Ukz8_vr8UU--PX7F0Ue6pIeClULaAC7hyMfzodp3LAwK9YND7IzBhUqaZvnMLGyk_uQzuq-s_YDO5RzZ3Vv2o61psYPVw0NdhIRjbrJlgl1byZu25ltKwyy8zXl25_k1_rkJZxp53ybU2zC8Kx1K0LfhRqFGo_juMWyXjOhRl7hVYQaY1r5FGqcU9xo9Xyx3XNdKUJmcifUBJlj2hleBXzcUq5QKftlKUAFfIXC_LeA40Bvu-ZX3KCAsRB4v-KU4X6XP1LMRugxzd4J_fPzOhsf5TOno7BQdt7fvFfStk6TnHpj-omJuZ90q4mQmRiFsuwvTzd5cNvhQ_DFdsLgdyQf3rvX__1iH9S_AAAA___YponS) on U.S. goods ahead of the country's 46% levy, set to begin April 9. Meanwhile, the European Union is expected to approve [retaliatory tariffs](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2OqzAUhJ_GdEbm2GBSuMj9octtbrElMnCcnMXYyHaC8vYrtt92Ps1o9H3cxpslT-H-z25oiosjCGi5UFzocbHk36OqFuMQAWWFptFKCRDQdNXDgJ6lXlDOoNvOQq_URapGXWBy_dxrW5E514QSuhFCKKibFq2QvZqwbWfsWqYELlRiqj8jhfJA59_xhame41Z58yhlz0xeGQwMhuM46i2HkzEYMPD7xGAIeOQTxuSXM37yjLhm_gxU3pwCd5Ry4bkkWpFPdl65Lbyk57bzYhM5d9Zt4tdr8zuu4S-TQ5xpYfLPr9MLHvk_pr1KZtkP8p5yp5kS982S_75ZftL2MvAVAAD__xk2b2A) on up to $28 billion worth of U.S. imports in the coming days, escalating trade tensions. President Trump is urging Americans to "[hang tough](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO4yAURb8Gd7YeD7CzBcXuRu6SZostLcCPhBlsLMCx8vcjTz_dlY50dHX-36abCTGsj7tZSFefJgRULcgWhmk2Ib4n2czaEyGJhjQfpAQE5H3z1NZcDP_F0YrZCVDe8sE4K5EMKAcSm6BPG0gYOABI7LgiA-IiLSnlqFdMAs2hptx9pLDWJ_n4Ti_KnUtLE_Wz1q0w8ZvhyHA8jqNztqx0lJMzHM_JcKx5X7Z2X8OLcjGxrSYH79tqPqkl78nVUyDGvS5TSXt2xMTVx7DZZPLMsD-BS2ultTJx3QtlhgrHv3_-3ekoTdbzdoQYQ-kHJuGxmBC_H9afir00fgUAAP__TLlvPQ)" as negotiations continue, saying the goal is to "bring back jobs and businesses like never before." Meanwhile, Dow Jones Industrial Average futures dropped 1,531 points, or 4%, on Sunday evening.
## Progressive Wave Targets Veteran Democrats
A wave of [young progressive Democrats](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LFy6yAQheGngU6eZVnQdUFxG3VOm1KzFiuJDAiPROzx22eUPvWZ8xX_5228ccppWz64SGhzHRHQdUAd9GPklN8j6RhmERSrJZieCBDQeL0GT9GwnZ0h76NDe5fe99crecvWXC3pFE4NCHoDAIQX44TB_qO7ODeJd4pAYmp1v3zVtLVV5vyuT9kvUy06h7W1x6Hsf4WDwqGtsqacz03hsNYim7wOhcPE5cFp2RQODq21BF2UUqed29G96_e2yN5xfNaJm5wHvYf4eKWc0-F7RbAUTr-ubn81eAb8CQAA___mxVzd) is challenging veteran incumbents, reflecting deep frustration within the party over its direction and aging leadership. The movement has drawn comparisons to the Tea Party uprising that reshaped the GOP more than a decade ago. In California, [Saikat Chakrabarti](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFutDAQhJ_GdEaLWeCucHH_H9FdmhQp0cbeA0fGRsZ3Fm8fkT7VjDSa0ej7vE93ct6F-Z1W1vkRJwWqk4AShsmS88eEldUPZsVtxboZEEGBavpq0Z1BhJY61fetba6mJ4tXBFamH3AwUDl9rgHC0AAAqrrpmKC94Bd3neG-EwhsXY6p_o4u5IUf_ogvTrWJa-X1kvO2i_Ym1CjUWEqp1z2cmVAjB_nchRoDl1O26F125rQUYl44yegtJ7nE587S8hpNoixtorJLkkd8hlluya2UDmkW8p7DzOnsJ3m7Nf_pwkm0YzTOivbt38mIy_7BaauStltx3ru9HwTCvJLzv5fzXwhfWv0EAAD__-Y8dFw), a former aide to Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, is running against former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, while Jake Rakov is challenging Rep. Brad Sherman. In Illinois, 26-year-old TikTok influencer [Kat Abughazaleh](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLtynTAQhp9GdDqzCHErVKShc9qUzFpaYGMhnZH22EOePoN7N3_1zX_787a-IUdO-288ycmWVwOm12A1jGtAjtdqm-A2IkNdQ64drQUDph2awxlq_Qa2NWM3dbadZt_POPp5I9xg7qeG3e0GFsYWAKx5tD0hdJN9p773NPTKAgWWXB5_Myc5aItX_qTy8PlsojtEnlV1v5RZlFm-66Rc5PiiKlTSTSmz3BnKLGBv6ZRZIgpVqZILU72BQV-ERecY9AeKxvfXfuA_jHToSvRRtWQduJbXU3SgM_uCwl5zQi-ck8aU8it5qtrntBeqlXPCqD2mwAH9pczSFBeeXxwj12FUFvYTOX7vkJ9-_XTmfwAAAP__6r98oQ) is challenging longtime Rep. Jan Schakowsky and calling for generational change.
## UConn Women Blow Out South Carolina
The UConn Huskies dominated the South Carolina Gamecocks [82-59 to win](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFutDAQhJ_GdKD13hrfX7j4G6QUlzYlWvACTox9At-he_uIKG26GY00-vR93PobhxjS_M6ruDLlHgFNDVSD7T2H-Oqp8m4SQblU4rQlAgTUbbU41JYQJpDJ0tVza7R49u0_w4a8HocquPMNCKwGAMJGG2G4XGkQY0ZpjSIQH0rems8cUllkiq_8lK0Z81pFt5Ry39Xlv8JOYXccRyP7PZ3jWfMqaa_HHKPMUg-8f0kZOEaF3cyrKOz63_jmFXYE2lIL1ijsHmNOqd7zoyz1yFuOIXG1OX8_Qoxhb60imFcO8Qek_CXm6fA7AAD__7bVZF0) the women's college basketball national championship, avenging their loss in the 2022 title game. It was a repeat of UConn's blowout win earlier this season when they snapped South Carolina's 71-game home winning streak. Claiming their****[12th national title](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00L2OpDAQBOCnMZmRMeYvcHAJ2Vx6IWpwM_RtYyPcM2jefjW7ySYbl-pTqf7dphsQU7z_hR29rGmyxjbaOG26KQDxa3JF8CuixbpAX3XOGWts1RabN90cumGFpq7noe9bF4Z5RZzd4LCvq1CQf2vGma4yxjhbVg2CqXs3Y9Ms2DbKGQwk6Sz_J4qy4cqv9MSzXNJesN9EjqzqP8qOyo7XdZVxAXiHyo5MT9SPI4BgVnacIX-gzMCsr7RjVHYMlbLjY0kx6jMxZ31AFp3TQza9wJmYIuiLoj5xSWfQlZXtu5z1Dy6CUIrAWkgYi9OH4yJmym2nnLnvQPy1V3777-ntZwAAAP__5OV1pg), the Huskies return to the top of the women's game for the first time since their four-year championship run from 2013 to 2016. Ayla Brown**,** a former Division I basketball player, _American Idol_ contestant, and future host of _The Flyover_ podcast--performed the national anthem before tipoff. [Watch her sing here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00D2PozAQxvFPA52jsWNeGhfX0OXaK9FgD-Bb40H2JFG-_Yrtt3_006P_v8f8wJhi3v7iQU5Wng2YToFVMMwBY_rMtg1uJTJ0b8npwVowYHTf7i7AaHS_6HHRflnCith1BkgHsCHAaNvoLg0sDBoArLnpjhDuo12o6zz1XWOBQhQut_8cs-y0pg-_qNw8H21yu8hZm_ufxkyNmVD2xLmeXKReg8ZMnlOijdSC9YtkwZQaM2WUyBmTwks81Ell5XJg9qRQ1NNzzqryU3blsXCKGZXf8Tgj57rHU214kAoF31WhCOWLa4sL5zumFGs_NBa2A2P6uSm_ZXs58x0AAP__ujN0SA).

Yummy candy, a fun toy, and crayons for drawing spring flowers--these delight children of all ages! But many inner-city children never experience the simple joy of receiving an Easter surprise. clean_body: At least have approached the Trump administration to negotiate new trade deals as a wave of international tariffs begins to take effect. | | | | | | Monday, April 7, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1969, the Defense Department's Advanced Research Projects Agency published the first digital document on a shared computer network, which is often regarded as the symbolic . Today, we're spotlighting 94-year-old Judith Viorst, who takes us through in a charming essay that proves personal style only gets better with age. Today's sponsor is the . This Easter, they're bringing joy to children in underserved communities--and you can be part of it. Join their mission today. | Trade Negotiations in Full Swing over Tariffs At least have approached the Trump administration to negotiate new trade deals as a wave of international tariffs begins to take effect. Vietnam, which sells far more to the U.S. than it buys in return--making it one of the largest contributors to America's trade gap--said Sunday that it is "ready" to on U.S. goods ahead of the country's 46% levy, set to begin April 9. Meanwhile, the European Union is expected to approve on up to $28 billion worth of U.S. imports in the coming days, escalating trade tensions. President Trump is urging Americans to "" as negotiations continue, saying the goal is to "bring back jobs and businesses like never before." Meanwhile, Dow Jones Industrial Average futures dropped 1,531 points, or 4%, on Sunday evening. Progressive Wave Targets Veteran Democrats A wave of is challenging veteran incumbents, reflecting deep frustration within the party over its direction and aging leadership. The movement has drawn comparisons to the Tea Party uprising that reshaped the GOP more than a decade ago. In California, , a former aide to Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, is running against former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, while Jake Rakov is challenging Rep. Brad Sherman. In Illinois, 26-year-old TikTok influencer is challenging longtime Rep. Jan Schakowsky and calling for generational change. UConn Women Blow Out South Carolina The UConn Huskies dominated the South Carolina Gamecocks the women's college basketball national championship, avenging their loss in the 2022 title game. It was a repeat of UConn's blowout win earlier this season when they snapped South Carolina's 71-game home winning streak. Claiming their**, the Huskies return to the top of the women's game for the first time since their four-year championship run from 2013 to 2016. Ayla Brown, a former Division I basketball player, American Idol contestant, and future host of The Flyover podcast--performed the national anthem before tipoff. . What's more, they may have never understood the true meaning of Easter: the joy of Christ's resurrection! You can change this for a child -- for an entire family. Your generosity can deliver a special Easter bag filled with goodies ... but it contains so much more. Each bag also includes a clear presentation of the gospel, designed especially for children! # The system is crumbling, protect your wealth or suffer the fallout.

Elon Musk has avoided two major financial crises before. He pulled Tesla and SpaceX back from the brink of collapse and built two of the most valuable companies in history.

Now, he’s sounding the alarm about America’s $36 trillion debt time bomb that could destroy the fabric of our society.

name: No Dem Left Behind PAC
subject: Trump’s Trade War: All Pain, No Plan
body:
The tariffs are already hurting Americans across the country, but Trump was on the golf course this weekend telling people to "hang tough."
| | | | | | [](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2x1dz9-w4sun7-kyd76rh1/)
---
# **Trump’s Trade War: All Pain, No Plan**
---
| [](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2x1dz9-w4sun8-kyd76rh2/)
---
| | | | Dear friend, Donald Trump is playing economic roulette with American livelihoods. After announcing a sweeping 10% global tariff on imports, Trump sent the markets into a tailspin. In just two days, the Dow dropped a staggering 2,200 points, and the S&P 500 lost 10%. That’s real money wiped out from retirement accounts, small businesses, and working families’ futures, all thanks to Trump’s ego-driven trade war. Even worse? Trump’s tariffs aren’t just targeting major economies. He’s imposing tariffs on remote territories like Heard Island and the McDonald Islands, places with no exports whatsoever. Whether it’s sheer ignorance or a performative stunt, it’s clear there’s no real strategy behind it. His own Commerce Secretary admits it’s not about trade, it’s about making sure “no countries are left off.” Translation: **MAGA only cares about causing chaos.** Trump had the audacity to tell the American people to “hang tough” this weekend, downplaying a market crash that was a direct consequence of his own reckless tariff policy. As if Americans should simply endure the fallout of his decisions, while he refuses to take responsibility for the chaos he unleashed. While families watched their retirement accounts shrink and small businesses braced for rising costs, Trump was nowhere to be found, **except on the golf course.** Meanwhile, his economic advisers were left to spin the disaster on national television, claiming the tariffs won’t impact consumers. MAGA Republicans are still clinging to the myth that this trade war is a strategy, when in reality, it’s reckless economic sabotage, and everyday Americans are going to end up paying the price. [**_This is what Trump’s leadership looks like: reckless decisions, economic instability, and a Republican Party too afraid to stop him. We need to be the voices calling this out, join us today._**](https://click.nodemleftbehind.com/click/gpnv-2x1dz9-w4sun9-kyd76rh3/) In Solidarity, The No Dem Left Behind Team
---

# Monday, April 7, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1886, a gunfight erupted in Laredo during a parade to celebrate the victory of the Botas ("Boots") over the Guaraches ("Sandals") in the recent city election. As many as 30 people were killed in the [Laredo Election Riot](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0zzGOgzAQheHT2B1oPB4wKVxsQ5dtt0RDbIMlgyOwQrj9ivSp_up90vu7D3eOKa7TLy_elnfIAwI2FVAFZnAc0zmQdHYEd1PSW2WIALXBTs627XDkxj-o0eyddtqwQQ5aOcJgRpLRXhgQGAVNB22tFCN1KoQALYDygsC7WPJWl9kX_-Y9pDO__FY_8iKTnUt57kL_COwF9sdx1GWfOa8prr7O2ySw_6wqx2c1nlcE9n4t21V9I7lZ9zxiSnFvjSCYFo7pg5evb18W_wMAAP__aFlWxw), and the U.S. military deployed several hundred infantry and cavalry to restore order. The Houston Cougars play the Florida Gators tonight for a chance to win their first men's basketball national championship. ## Texans Join 'Hands Off!' Protests
Demonstrators around Texas participated in the nationwide “Hands Off!” protests against President Donald Trump and DOGE head Elon Musk on Saturday. In Houston, more than [5,000 people gathered at City Hall](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2ynDAMhZ_GdGaEMD-3cJGGbtOmZLRYBiVem8HOsvv2GXbS3kaNPumbc37d5htJkLj-pAfb8vJpRsBOg9EwzI4kvGdTOXsH99VUbJvBGMB2wLHaLLov4513Szdwh-Qctv6-dNR7NDyMbSX2egYGhga6Efq6aQjN2HjvoQdoWBlgJyUdddm48IuyD-_05KNe0qMKditlz6r9oXBSOJ3nWS9Blj-4pb-5pHhRCqfIZ1Y4hbRQUDhdToUTmGv0Cqf_tP6dJGYdqUiKpzjWG0WXdfJe70cqnEvWp5RNl-viszsohLfEVdNKEnPRnh0fFPSegizCl7c6rNtPCUFyPygD64MkfAKUbxt9WvwXAAD__3DPe2Y), displaying signs with messages like “Democracy Is Not For Sale” and “Hands Off Our Healthcare and Courts.” Protests were also held in the nearby towns of Conroe and The Woodlands. Democratic lawmakers joined protesters at the Texas Capitol in Austin, where law enforcement estimated up to [7,000 people had gathered](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOpiAUhZ8Gu2suCIoFxTZ2s-2WBuSiZFCM8P_OvP3G6ac7yUm-k_P9-5g_bEzxWP_anUz9CnkWKBSgBBxmb2P6nmXjjUM_8oYMH6RE0Q1CN5sJS3C690pJTaN3xJfgBheCCKR7N9ommgeGEgeOSmPfcm6F1DyEgD0iJyaRfKz5autGlb5sCek7v-lql7w3yWy1noV1f5iYmJju-24_3y96SiYme9W4JGJiOuguTExnTrHG5Yk_LFjsGWtOsNnDF8ghwHnlSqWCXW08SoXzohI9HRV8PmzyUK_XfgKlfMD-Kp_g8_osiH6EQILLATWE0SHIpVMwdprAjU55JCWVoOYy_rxjSrH0A5O47jamnzP1V7tvI_4HAAD__zzffaQ). "_I feel like everyone's rights are being taken away_ ,” said one demonstrator. ## Second Death in Measles Outbreak
A second child [died at a Lubbock hospital](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOwjAQBb8m6RytF8dJChfX0HHtldEGr8kKhyDbYPj7E9df94qn0Wh-TvOJJMrt8k0bu_IK-4yAvQKjYJg9SXzPpvVuAT_plp0ejAE8DDi2q1uI9DQtMLHuB-pt8D5YHYzFYKexH1txHxgYGDT0I9hOa0Iz6hACWADNjQH2UvbUlZULvyiH-N6fnLrzvrXRraXcc3P4avDY4LHW2t245sp8_RwaPG5MOXJW-6Msiemq6LaXlZM6rxK98sJZnSlzVvkuV1ZPSY_PTkw-K4TegsY2OX-vEqNkOzQGLhtJ_DMo_yZ5OvwNAAD__4qQZM0) on Thursday after contracting measles, which has infected at least 481 people since the current outbreak began in West Texas in January. The 8-year-old girl reportedly died from “measles pulmonary failure” at University Medical Center, [according to a statement](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO6yAQRb8Gd6AZgoNTULzGXV67pTUxQ4wWQ2RInPz9at1vfa_Okc7XdbpSTDHf_9PKrr1DmTToXoKRYCdPMX0m03l3A3_Bjh1aY0CfrB66xQXbXxD9hU4eBkZCMr0l64cbztQzdNH9wsCARegHOCtE0mbAEAKcAZCFAfaxlU21hRu_qYb0KS_e1FzWLrmltUcVp39Cj0KP-74rX5eqjqe6l5fQY-a9Skq8tSr0eCySci7PPHOVleeSvfRMbZErU01cZXm228b03W3OP_aYUqxnKwzcV4rpMLc_U7yc_gkAAP__hA9jBg) from the Department of State Health Services. The statement added that she “was not vaccinated and had no reported underlying conditions.” Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. [visited with the girl's family](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOwyAQBb8Gd1jLeo2dguIad7n2SovAEqPgODJcSP7-5PTXvOZJM9L8nOezjSner992ZVNeYZsRsJdAEobZ25jeMzXeXMCfVMNGDUSA3YBjsxjqQA_smPveBerIKgu6JzeC1RD6UxPNAQOCQUE_gm6VskijCiGABlAsCNjHsu1tWbjwy-aQ3tuT99Zta5PMUsoji-5L4CRwqrW2N3fxxylwOtgCJ6BjtMAps9vuXrolJi9rLItc2ebEWfrIWVbORX4kcvstl53tTeDU7MY_akwpZj0IgutqY_roy789ngb_AgAA__-bg2DT) yesterday and said he is working with state and local health officials to address the outbreak. In a social media post, he said vaccination is the “most effective way to prevent the spread of measles.”
## Fort Worth Art Festival Returns
The state’s largest art festival is [coming to Fort Worth](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0ULuu4yAU_BrcHeuA8augiFZyd7fYZkuLaw4xWgwRkOD8_crpbzUjzUOj-fu1fmnnXbj_1gepctq4ChQ9oAQcV6Odf6-yMeobzcwbUnyUEkU3iqnZ1TDjNBv6NmIwop8l9UO3dZvs-2HaaMPGqasMJY4c-wmHlnMt5MSttTggcmISybgSU1t2KnTqbP07vii1Wzwar_ZSHpl1NyYWJpZaa3vkcGlMLBTgmZlYAtULPtzEGkqsAWxMBWpMZYcSoZLf4kGgU3G5ZLApHqC3FHOGZ4ZAZ4FK9O-KgQ7hqf3lzWApF_fSnolFJ7jd-C_zJ59NUuZRnfcuDyOTeD-085_F5ccLX0r8DwAA__8SHXYX) this weekend. Spanning 18 downtown blocks, the 38th annual Main Street Fort Worth Arts Festival will feature 219 artists from all over the country and offer multiple musical performances and dining options. Selected from over 1,000 applicants, the participants will showcase [$4 million worth of art](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0j7GO6yAQRb8GOqJhmEBSULzGXV67pUVsiJGwiWBkJ3-_Svqt79U5Oj-38RZyydvjf1ij51eqIwKeFZACN84hl_dIcvZ3mK9aRq8dEaBxeJGL15bAWbwipDudnTFI1iYX9JTCRBBl9h8YEDgN5wvYk9YB6aJTSmABdBQEcc5c24mXyPEVeirvusd2muoqi1-Yn12YfwIHgcN0GPMZBA5bPLrAodQpFIHDGvKmOqtUG6ujNl5UaNxVip3z_r3I5ufnkUvJ3TpB8FhDLl8N_9m9e_wNAAD__-f6WUs) in categories including ceramics, glass, jewelry, leather, painting, and photography. Melanie Summers, a Fort Worth artist who is legally blind, will exhibit pieces created using an ice cube, dye, and cloth. More than 50 performances will be provided by a[ diverse lineup of bands](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0j7luxCAUAL8GOqzHZZyCIo27TZvSwhxrJGxW-MnH30ebfvvRjOb3MT1cLnl7_rg1WrxSnQQIzUAxMFNwudyTosHOEL44jZYbpUBIIwa62CFJIxN4rZOL0s88SvAS5t4LpYPmNNu3DBQYDnqAvuPcCTXwlBL0ADwSBTFkrK3DJWK83J7KXY_YOl9XWuyC-NqJ_CZiJGJMeGIN7u56t_qM9xsiYnQNWYo75sOVneWNhXpuWM-NpdqQnbXhwvCizYbXmUvJe2-Igufqcvnv4Mfxw4q_AAAA__-lwlxk) over the four-day event. Five food courts will serve barbecue, Tex-Mex, gourmet burgers, and international cuisine. Kids' activities will include creating nature journals, making leaf rubbings, and even flying drones. The event is free, but food and drinks must be purchased on-site.

On this day in 1886, a gunfight erupted in Laredo during a parade to celebrate the victory of the Botas ("Boots") over the Guaraches ("Sandals") in the recent city election. As many as 30 people were killed in the , and the U.S. military deployed several hundred infantry and cavalry to restore order. The Houston Cougars play the Florida Gators tonight for a chance to win their first men's basketball national championship. for details. Today’s sponsor is , offering an investment opportunity in their pre-IPO offering. Abilene ☀️ 69/31° | Amarillo ☀️ 65/32° | Austin ☀️ 70/39° | Brownsville 🌤️ 76/55° | Corpus Christi ☀️ 76/50° | DFW ☀️ 65/37° | El Paso ☀️ 72/39° | Houston 🌤️ 67/46° | Laredo ☀️ 78/47° | Lubbock ☀️ 68/33° | Midland ☀️ 71/37° | San Antonio ☀️ 73/40° | Tyler 🌤️ 63/38° | Waco ☀️ 65/34° | Texans Join 'Hands Off!' Protests Demonstrators around Texas participated in the nationwide “Hands Off!” protests against President Donald Trump and DOGE head Elon Musk on Saturday. In Houston, more than , displaying signs with messages like “Democracy Is Not For Sale” and “Hands Off Our Healthcare and Courts.” Protests were also held in the nearby towns of Conroe and The Woodlands. Democratic lawmakers joined protesters at the Texas Capitol in Austin, where law enforcement estimated up to . "I feel like everyone's rights are being taken away ,” said one demonstrator. Protesters also gathered in , , , and . More than 600,000 people attended one of 1,200 similar protests across the country. Second Death in Measles Outbreak A second child on Thursday after contracting measles, which has infected at least 481 people since the current outbreak began in West Texas in January. The 8-year-old girl reportedly died from “measles pulmonary failure” at University Medical Center, from the Department of State Health Services. The statement added that she “was not vaccinated and had no reported underlying conditions.” Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. yesterday and said he is working with state and local health officials to address the outbreak. In a social media post, he said vaccination is the “most effective way to prevent the spread of measles.” Fort Worth Art Festival Returns The state’s largest art festival is this weekend. Spanning 18 downtown blocks, the 38th annual Main Street Fort Worth Arts Festival will feature 219 artists from all over the country and offer multiple musical performances and dining options. Selected from over 1,000 applicants, the participants will showcase in categories including ceramics, glass, jewelry, leather, painting, and photography. Melanie Summers, a Fort Worth artist who is legally blind, will exhibit pieces created using an ice cube, dye, and cloth. More than 50 performances will be provided by a over the four-day event. Five food courts will serve barbecue, Tex-Mex, gourmet burgers, and international cuisine. Kids' activities will include creating nature journals, making leaf rubbings, and even flying drones. The event is free, but food and drinks must be purchased on-site. | Flying together with our sponsor Elon Musk Dreams, Mode Mobile Delivers As Elon Musk puts it, “Apple used to really bring out products that would blow people’s minds.” Those days are long gone, and a new smartphone company is stepping up to deliver the mind-blowing moments we've been missing. , and you can still make an investment in their pre-IPO offering. | **100 RESPONSES NEEDED FROM 22122 BEFORE MIDNIGHT TONIGHT
Peter, we're not going to lie – Robert F Kennedy Jr. leading the future of health care has us scared.

Take a look at some recent headlines: [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tad7ef931-be8c-49b7-ab1b-3b1879d208dd/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) RFK Jr. and Trump's unqualified GOP cronies have shown us nothing that would "Make America Healthy Again." Instead, they’ve laid out plans to dismantle our health care system: ❌ CUTTING Medicaid benefits
❌ SLASHING critical medical and cancer research
❌ FIRING thousands of important federal workers
❌ ALLOWING an anti-science, conspiracy theorist to be a 'yes man' to the MAGA agenda Meanwhile, Democrats are on the front line fighting to protect and expand health care access, safeguard reproductive rights, lower prescription drug prices, and ensure Americans can keep their Medicaid benefits. We can't let RFK Jr., Trump, or any MAGA extremist threaten the future of our healthcare. They're a danger to our health and safety, and we will need to support Democrats who will fight for this right. | | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0be59782-5d1e-46ee-af8c-95ef6e497c72/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: History Facts
subject: The strange Victorian photo trend
body:
Though it may seem shocking to us today, taking and displaying postmortem photographs was a popular practice in European and American life during the Victoria era.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68epoued5n57lq0t0unb1al5cwjwz6p5jvalermldonnt1jcxq1xcde5pjhcii5wj75spxry29562ry4ix8qqb6m6iz1er2tqyiz6mv09ehkw3zvgywdxjxg6nizt0jpxdiwk3kf3ruufoapm&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=suchic&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
---
# [People in the Victorian era frequently took photos of the dead.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=suchic&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [Arts & Culture](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=suchic&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=suchic&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| T hough it may seem shocking to us today, taking and displaying postmortem photographs was a [popular practice](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=suchic&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) in European and American life during the Victoria era. In a time before modern sanitation, vaccines, and antibiotics, death was commonplace, and making images of the dead was part of mourning rituals. Postmortem portraiture — in the form of drawings and paintings — had long been available to those who could afford it, but the invention of photography [democratized](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=suchic&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) the trend.
---
While we tend to associate death with the morbid or grotesque today, many of these images were beautiful and emotional remembrances. The dead were often depicted in their beds or coffins accompanied by flowers (such as lilies and forget-me-nots) or keepsakes. If the deceased was a small child, they were held or propped up by family members. These photos were often displayed in parlors and family photo albums alongside pictures of the living, or they might be carried in lockets.
Postmortem photography reached its peak in the 1860s and 1870s, but began to fade away by the early 20th century, and had all but disappeared by the 1930s. By then, the cultural attitude toward death and mourning had changed. It was less likely that people would pass away at home and be taken care of by family members, and more likely that death was seen as a matter for hospitals and funeral homes. The mass casualties of World War I also encouraged a turn away from elaborate Victorian-era mourning customs. For one thing, families simply didn’t have time to mourn so many losses, and these wartime casualties often took place far from home. Yet today, postmortem photographs have become a treasured item for many collectors, who see great tenderness in these attempts at immortality.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=suchic&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=suchic&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
By clicking a link in this email, you're agreeing to our [Terms of Use](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=2kh5r9m0gvx3yot7g5nhew0zeloqmt63y3gzd8k0adeoxew1kju3uifhy6iou1r8g2yabk7mqyja4c334bev7yqrzhdsmn0uv3gim3n6n3ul30tdjb3b5uxcq8kuzh04dg7adeyy9810h336vc23unqp7iskbbt5lqrn973w35zj2&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=suchic&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) and [Privacy Policy](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=463ko1dghovdsrodlhyh4xecnddcs00a1rd282qzew24di0getw64t8z7zk8vy3xxbcghwwjmo947kcn751383tj827uymi9d2kjno33mrvwfo4mulhf26pmpuesnf7chb35fnazi3pvhnq1pg4x9azi17strd8ovd1nxfsmwkgoal1a&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=suchic&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com).
